task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'CustomerLifetimeValue' query with the required columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a saved query named 'CustomerLifetimeValue' in the LibreOffice Base Queries pane, or an open query design/execution window showing the columns: CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Country, OrderCount, TotalSpent, and AvgOrderValue.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'CustomerTier' table with the correct schema.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a table named 'CustomerTier' in the Tables pane, or a table design view showing the columns: TierId (primary key), TierName, MinSpend, MaxSpend, and Description.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least four rows of data are inserted into the 'CustomerTier' table with realistic thresholds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a data view of the 'CustomerTier' table showing at least 4 rows (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum). The MinSpend and MaxSpend values should logically cover the actual spending range of the database (approx $36 to $50).

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'CustomerTierAssignment' query.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a saved query named 'CustomerTierAssignment' in the Queries pane, or an execution window showing customers joined with their assigned tiers based on their total spending.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Customer Analysis' report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a report named 'Customer Analysis' in the Reports pane of LibreOffice Base, or an open report document presenting the customer segmentation data.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the LibreOffice Base GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LibreOffice Base interface (menus, toolbars, query/table design views) rather than just terminal windows executing SQLite commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Chinook database data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Query results or table views should display genuine Chinook data (e.g., real customer names, invoice totals) rather than mock data, empty tables, or hardcoded text.

integrity.description: Verify the report was created using LibreOffice Base's reporting tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report should appear in the LibreOffice Base Reports section or be generated via the LibreOffice Report Builder, not just a manually typed text document or spreadsheet.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Chinook database contains 59 customers and 412 invoices. Actual customer total spending ranges from a minimum of $36.64 to a maximum of $49.62, with a median of $37.62. All customers have 6-7 invoices. When evaluating the 'CustomerTier' table's 'realistic spend thresholds', look for ranges that meaningfully segment the $36.64 to $49.62 span (e.g., $36-$39, $39-$42, etc.). If an agent uses generic ranges like $0-$100 or $100-$1000, it fails to properly segment this specific dataset, as all customers would fall into a single tier.
